div.p: la´mi = to dip (hide) in brains (a tanning process)
li´su = to draw (on cigarette or pipe) to smoke
ta´poluhkik 'uku´cunik'a´htca = I'm going to break the bottle
wä´ha = to light (of a fire, not tr.)
ta´˳ic wä´hak'ara = the fire is lighting
ta´˳ic wä´hə'aki = the fire was lighting
taRkuk 'una´ha'a´rani = I'm leaning against a tree
ta´wickatahi tita´hin'ahkini = I strung my bow
tita´hihtanik'ahtca = I'm going to string my bow
'uwi´ckatahic tahə'utak'ahtca = he's going to string his bow
tiwi´ckatahic ta´hən'ätak'ahtca = she's going to string her bow
'į´wickata´hi ta´hə'inak'ahtca = we're going to string our bow
'ų´wickatahi ta´hə'unak'ahtca = they (2 m.) are going to string their bow
sä´mą pro sį´wickatahi n ta´hitak'ahtca v.I
wi´ya = to throw, to throw off
hi´pu˳ą ya´wą hi˳a´ka = I'm fixing to dance
lɔtawą ya´wą hi˳a´ką = I'm fixing to run
Notes on Phonetics in the connection with the Objctival Prefixes (pp.
Verb stems beginning in voiceless stops
cu´tci = to shoot (with bow and arrow)
Verb stems beginning in voiced consonants
y, w, m v.I, n , l, r
'a´maisa´hkų = on either side, on both sides, on all sides

tr.td: when it got there she came
when it got there she came

div.p: Coming he set down and ate
He came, sat down and ate
'ukɔ´hpaka´ni = I head him (I get ahead of him?)
alternates between mi´hku v.II + mi´htu or 'umi´tuwą

tr.td: when she came close to catch him
when she came close to catch him
to chase her away he [wanted, intended, tried
to chase her away he [wanted, intended, tried
they surrounded her/he placed around her
they surrounded her/he placed around her
you are going to shoot and kill him
you are going to shoot and kill him
he lassoed it (hooked it with the rope)
he lassoed it (hooked it with the rope)

div.p: ‎‎ even if he says he's me, don't let him go

tr.td: If I pull him + throw him
If I pull him + throw him
it (the tree) he got down from (the tree)
it (the tree) he got down from (the tree)

div.p: timi´rakani I show it to her v.I
ha´˳ic kä´ra pa´t'aki = she fell backwards
te´hni´yuc ha´p'aki pa´t'aki = she missed her breath when she fell
ni´'ähkį la´puya adv wihä´taha you didn't hear v.II
ti´hikatä´pą adv ta´ya 'ɔ´patä´rą deer-leaf lard n
hį˳a´ti conn 'u´wį pro ta´paruc n wi´čį v.I
doesn't know how they used to
have gotten them from the different animals
some way, but he doesn't know how they
the dances are named Chicken Dance,
